JUDGE DONNA J. HITCHENS, CLERK NICOLE OLCOMENDY, REPORTER PATRICIA VAN STEENBERGE. MASTER CALENDAR MOTION CALENDAR ON FEB-21-2002 IN DEPT. 206, MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL &/OR SETTLEMENT CONF. W/ STIPULATION TO TAKE TRIAL OFF CALENDAR ATTACHED GRANTED. TRIAL CONTINUED TO AUG-19-2002.
PLAN I STATUS CONFERENCE DATE: HEARING SET FOR OCT-26-2001 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 212

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION, SEPARATE STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY HEARING SET FOR JAN-24-2002 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301
OPPOSITION TO MEMO OF P&A IN SUPPORT OF DEFTS MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF JIMENEZ, JOSEPHINE L

LAW AND MOTION 301, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT CONTINUED FROM JAN-24-2002 TO LAW AND MOTION AT APR-25-2002, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301. MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING -- MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT GRANTED -NO TRIABLE ISSUE THAT SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT COVERS FUTURE EMPLOYMENT ISSUES. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING DENIED - TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO HOUSING AUTHORITY OBLIGATION TO MAKE RETIREMENT CONTRIBUTION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, IS GRANTED - NO TRIABLE ISSUE RE: COLLECTIVE BARGAINING AGREEMENT. HOWEVER, THE COURT STAYED ITS RULING ON THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION SINCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ON RETIREMENT BENEFITS IS GOING TO ARBITRATION. COUNSEL IS TO REPORT BACK TO THIS DEPARTMENT ON A P R I L 2 5, 2 0 0 2 FOR A STATUS REPORT. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE. REPORTED BY ERIC THRONE. CSR 7855 (GPS)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2002 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2002 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. Tentative ruling adopted -- defendant San Francisco Housing Authority's Motion for Summary Judgment: OFF CALENDAR per request of moving party on April 18, 2002. (JJM) Eric Throne, CSR 7855

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-21-2002 Continued To Aug-19-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-21-2002 Granted Per Written Order

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2002 Continued To Apr-25-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William F. Terheyden, Esq. of LITTLER MENDELSON 433-1940 for defendant SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY moving for summary judgment/summary adjudication
Terence D. Edwards, Esq. of the Law Offices of THOMAS J. BRANDI 989-1800 for Plaintiff opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- Motion for Summary Judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to first cause of action for breach of contract granted -no triable issue that settlement agreement covers future employment issues. Motion for summary adjudication as to the second cause of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing denied - triable issue as to Housing Authority obligation to make retirement contribution. Motion for summary adjudication of the third cause of action, petition to compel arbitraion, is granted - no triable issue re: collective bargaining agreement.
* However, the Court stayed its ruling on the Second Cause of Action since the settlement agreement on retirement benefits is going to arbitration. Counsel is to report back to this Department on A P R I L 2 5, 2 0 0 2 for a status report.
(JJM/???) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

STATUS CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-05-2001 Set For Trial On Feb-25-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
